title: podman image prune fails on untagged images used by containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
`podman` fails to prune images if it encounters an untagged image used by a container.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Pull an image: `podman pull docker.io/library/ubuntu`

2. Create a container out of it: `podman run docker.io/library/ubuntu`

3. Untag the image: `podman rmi docker.io/library/ubuntu`

4. Attempt to prune the images: `podman image prune`

**Describe the results you received:**
`podman` returns an error:
```
Error: failed to prune image: Image used by cf7ab1d066b4eb79b48b46469f3faacc41eb84682806d7305000cbe97658d9fc: image is in use by a container
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Prune to succeed. Docker, for example, successfully avoids cleaning up untagged-but-still-used images.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.5-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.4
  podman version: 1.4.5-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.2
  Conmon:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.0.0-dev, commit: 6f177c68ed989a6cc14ddbf8cec421819bb46401'
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "19.04"
  MemFree: 402661376
  MemTotal: 8300957696
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 2146168832
  SwapTotal: 2147479552
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: file
  hostname: eric-ubuntu
  kernel: 5.0.0-21-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 8h 27m 41.72s (Approximately 0.33 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search: null
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 30
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 77
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical Ubuntu box.

comments.body:
This looks like a logic error in [image.GetPruneImages](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/image/prune.go#L20). `podman` should ignore any image that has a container while `all` flag should only control the `Names` condition. So it would:
- Delete unused images with no tag when `all=false`
- Delete all unused images when `all=true`
Nevermind - looks like in normal flow `podman rmi` doesn't let you untag an image that has a container. I somehow got my podman storage in a weird state where I had a dangling stopped container.
